title: webapp Helm Chart Values
description: JSON Schema for the webapp Helm chart values.yaml

repository.description: Container image registry and repository path

tag.description: Image tag. Defaults to the chart appVersion when empty.

imagePullSecrets.description: List of image pull secrets for private registries

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing registry credentials

nameOverride.description: Override the release name used in resource names

fullnameOverride.description: Override the full resource name, disabling the default prefix logic

replicaCount.description: Number of pod replicas. Ignored when autoscaling.enabled is true.

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests and limits for the container

requests.description: Minimum resources guaranteed to the container

limits.description: Maximum resources the container can consume

enabled.description: Enable or disable the HPA. When enabled, replicaCount is ignored.

minReplicas.description: Minimum number of replicas the HPA will maintain

maxReplicas.description: Maximum number of replicas the HPA can scale to

targetCPUUtilizationPercentage.description: Target average CPU utilization percentage (relative to request) that triggers scaling

targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage.description: Target average memory utilization percentage that triggers scaling

behavior.description: HPA scaling behavior policies controlling scale-up and scale-down velocity

scaleUp.description: Policy governing how quickly the HPA can add replicas

stabilizationWindowSeconds.description: Seconds to wait after a scale-up before evaluating again

value.description: Value for the policy (number of pods or percentage)

periodSeconds.description: Period in seconds over which the policy is evaluated

scaleDown.description: Policy governing how quickly the HPA can remove replicas

stabilizationWindowSeconds.description: Seconds to wait after a scale-down before evaluating again

value.description: Value for the policy (number of pods or percentage)

periodSeconds.description: Period in seconds over which the policy is evaluated

service.description: Kubernetes Service configuration for exposing the application

targetPort.description: Port on the container that the Service forwards traffic to

portName.description: Named port exposed by the container, referenced by probes and ServiceMonitor

annotations.description: Additional annotations to add to the Service (e.g., for cloud load balancer configuration)

ingress.description: Ingress configuration for external HTTP/HTTPS access

enabled.description: Enable or disable the Ingress resource

className.description: Ingress class name (e.g., nginx, traefik, alb)

annotations.description: Additional annotations for the Ingress (e.g., cert-manager issuer, rate-limiting)

hosts.description: List of host rules for the Ingress

host.description: Hostname for the Ingress rule (e.g., webapp.example.com)

paths.description: List of path rules under this host

secretName.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the TLS certificate and key

hosts.description: Hostnames covered by this TLS certificate

probes.description: Health probe configuration for liveness, readiness, and startup checks

liveness.description: Liveness probe - restarts the container if it fails

port.description: Port name or number to probe

initialDelaySeconds.description: Seconds to wait before starting the probe

periodSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to run the probe

timeoutSeconds.description: Seconds after which the probe times out

failureThreshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before the container is restarted

successThreshold.description: Number of consecutive successes required after a failure

readiness.description: Readiness probe - removes the pod from Service endpoints if it fails

port.description: Port name or number to probe

initialDelaySeconds.description: Seconds to wait before starting the probe

periodSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to run the probe

timeoutSeconds.description: Seconds after which the probe times out

failureThreshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before the pod is removed from endpoints

successThreshold.description: Number of consecutive successes to mark the pod ready again

startup.description: Startup probe - delays liveness/readiness probes until the app starts. Allows slow-starting containers.

port.description: Port name or number to probe

initialDelaySeconds.description: Seconds to wait before starting the probe

periodSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to run the probe

timeoutSeconds.description: Seconds after which the probe times out

failureThreshold.description: Maximum attempts before the container is killed (failureThreshold * periodSeconds = max startup time)

successThreshold.description: Number of consecutive successes to consider the startup complete

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context. These defaults comply with the 'restricted' Pod Security Standards profile.

runAsNonRoot.description: Require the pod to run as a non-root user

runAsUser.description: UID to run the pod processes as

runAsGroup.description: GID to run the pod processes as

fsGroup.description: Group ID applied to all volumes mounted by the pod

seccompProfile.description: Seccomp profile to apply to the pod

securityContext.description: Container-level security context applied to the main container

allowPrivilegeEscalation.description: Prevent the container from gaining more privileges than its parent process

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: Mount the container root filesystem as read-only

runAsNonRoot.description: Require the container to run as a non-root user

runAsUser.description: UID to run the container process as

capabilities.description: Linux capabilities to add or drop

drop.description: Capabilities to drop (use ALL to drop everything)

add.description: Capabilities to add (avoid unless strictly necessary)

create.description: Create a dedicated ServiceAccount for the pods

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the ServiceAccount (e.g., for IAM role binding)

name.description: ServiceAccount name override. Defaults to the chart fullname if empty.

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Opt out of automounting the API token into pods when not needed

podDisruptionBudget.description: Pod Disruption Budget to ensure availability during voluntary disruptions (node drains, upgrades)

enabled.description: Enable or disable the PodDisruptionBudget resource

minAvailable.description: Minimum number or percentage of pods that must remain available during a disruption

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number or percentage of pods that can be unavailable during a disruption

enabled.description: Enable or disable the ServiceMonitor resource. Requires the Prometheus Operator CRD.

namespace.description: Namespace for the ServiceMonitor. Defaults to the release namespace when empty.

scrapeTimeout.description: Prometheus scrape timeout (must be less than interval)

path.description: HTTP path where the application exposes Prometheus metrics

port.description: Named port to scrape for metrics

additionalLabels.description: Extra labels to match the Prometheus operator selector

metricRelabelings.description: Metric relabeling rules applied after scraping

relabelings.description: Target relabeling rules applied before scraping

env.description: Extra environment variables injected into the container

valueFrom.description: Reference to a Secret or ConfigMap key (mutually exclusive with value)

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volume mounts appended to the container (in addition to the default tmp mount)

extraVolumes.description: Extra volumes appended to the pod spec (in addition to the default tmp volume)

podAnnotations.description: Additional annotations applied to pods (e.g., for Prometheus scraping, Vault injection)

nodeSelector.description: Node selector constraints to control which nodes pods are scheduled on

tolerations.description: Tolerations to allow pods to schedule on tainted nodes

affinity.description: Affinity rules merged with the default anti-affinity for pod spreading

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Topology spread constraints to distribute pods across failure domains (overrides the default)
